001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
023
024import java.io.*;
025import java.lang.reflect.*;
026import java.nio.charset.*;
027import java.time.*;
028import java.time.Duration;
029import java.time.temporal.*;
030import java.util.*;
031
032import javax.xml.datatype.*;
033
034import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
036import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
037import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
038import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
039import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
040import org.apache.juneau.marshall.collections.*;
041import org.apache.juneau.marshall.httppart.*;
042import org.apache.juneau.marshall.objecttools.*;
043import org.apache.juneau.marshall.swap.*;
044import org.apache.juneau.marshall.utils.*;
045
046/**
047 * Session object that lives for the duration of a single use of {@link Parser}.
048 *
049 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
050 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe and is typically discarded after one use.
051 * </ul>
052 *
053 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
054 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SerializersAndParsers">Serializers and Parsers</a>
055
056 * </ul>
057 */
058@SuppressWarnings({
059   "java:S115",  // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
060   "rawtypes",   // Raw types necessary for generic type handling throughout parser session
061   "unchecked",  // Type erasure requires unchecked casts throughout parser session
062   "resource" // pipe field holds the per-parse ParserPipe whose lifecycle is managed by the session, not owned as a standalone field.
063})
064public class ParserSession extends MarshallingSession {
065
066   // Property name constants
067   private static final String PROP_javaMethod = "javaMethod";
068   private static final String PROP_listener = "listener";
069   private static final String PROP_outer = "outer";
070   private static final String PROP_schema = "schema";
071   private static final String PROP_trimStrings = "trimStrings";
072   private static final String PROP_nulls = "nulls";
073   private static final String PROP_ParserSession_javaMethod = "ParserSession.javaMethod";
074   private static final String PROP_ParserSession_outer = "ParserSession.outer";
075   private static final String PROP_ParserSession_schema = "ParserSession.schema";
076   private static final String PROP_ParserSession_trimStrings = "ParserSession.trimStrings";
077   private static final String PROP_ParserSession_nulls = "ParserSession.nulls";
078
079   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
080   private static final String ARG_ctx = "ctx";
081
082   /**
083    * Builder class.
084    */
085   @SuppressWarnings({
086      "java:S119" // 'SELF' (CRTP self-type) is intentional and clearer than a single-letter name.
087   })
088   public abstract static class Builder<SELF extends Builder<SELF>> extends MarshallingSession.Builder<SELF> {
089
090      private HttpPartSchema schema;
091      private Method javaMethod;
092      private Object outer;
093      private boolean trimStrings;
094      private Nulls nulls;
095      private Parser ctx;
096
097      /**
098       * Constructor
099       *
100       * @param ctx The context creating this session.
101       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
102       */
103      protected Builder(Parser ctx) {
104         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx).getMarshallingContext());
105         this.ctx = ctx;
106         mediaTypeDefault(ctx.getPrimaryMediaType());
107         trimStrings = ctx.isTrimStrings();
108         nulls = ctx.getNulls();
109      }
110
111      @Override
112      public ParserSession build() {
113         return new ParserSession(this);
114      }
115
116      /**
117       * The java method that called this serializer, usually the method in a REST servlet.
118       *
119       * @param value
120       *    The new property value.
121       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
122       * @return This object.
123       */
124      public SELF javaMethod(Method value) {
125         javaMethod = value;
126         return self();
127      }
128
129
130      /**
131       * The outer object for instantiating top-level non-static inner classes.
132       *
133       * @param value
134       *    The new property value.
135       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (no outer object will be used, suitable for static or top-level classes).
136       * @return This object.
137       */
138      public SELF outer(Object value) {
139         outer = value;
140         return self();
141      }
142
143      @Override /* Overridden from Builder */
144      public SELF property(String key, Object value) {
145         if (key == null) {
146            super.property(key, value);
147            return self();
148         }
149         switch (key) {
150            case PROP_javaMethod, PROP_ParserSession_javaMethod:
151               return javaMethod(cvt(value, Method.class));
152            case PROP_outer, PROP_ParserSession_outer:
153               return outer(value);
154            case PROP_schema, PROP_ParserSession_schema:
155               return schema(cvt(value, HttpPartSchema.class));
156            case PROP_trimStrings, PROP_ParserSession_trimStrings:
157               return trimStrings(cvt(value, Boolean.class));
158            case PROP_nulls, PROP_ParserSession_nulls:
159               return nulls(cvt(value, Nulls.class));
160            default:
161               super.property(key, value);
162               return self();
163         }
164      }
165
166      /**
167       * Trim parsed strings.
168       *
169       * @param value The new value for this setting.
170       * @return This object.
171       */
172      public SELF trimStrings(boolean value) {
173         trimStrings = value;
174         return self();
175      }
176
177      /**
178       * Per-session override for the parser's null-coercion policy.
179       *
180       * @param value The new value for this setting.  {@code null} is treated as {@link Nulls#NOT_SET}.
181       * @return This object.
182       * @see Parser.Builder#nulls(Nulls)
183       * @since 10.0.0
184       */
185      public SELF nulls(Nulls value) {
186         nulls = value == null ? Nulls.NOT_SET : value;
187         return self();
188      }
189
190      /**
191       * HTTP-part schema.
192       *
193       * <p>
194       * Used for schema-based serializers and parsers to define additional formatting.
195       *
196       * @param value
197       *    The new value for this property.
198       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will not set the value, keeps existing schema or remains <jk>null</jk>).
199       * @return This object.
200       */
201      public SELF schema(HttpPartSchema value) {
202         if (nn(value))
203            schema = value;
204         return self();
205      }
206
207      /**
208       * Same as {@link #schema(HttpPartSchema)} but doesn't overwrite the value if it is already set.
209       *
210       * @param value
211       *    The new value for this property.
212       *    <br>If <jk>null</jk>, the value will not be set (keeps existing schema or remains <jk>null</jk>).
213       * @return This object.
214       */
215      public SELF schemaDefault(HttpPartSchema value) {
216         if (nn(value) && schema == null)
217            schema = value;
218         return self();
219      }
220
221   }
222
223   /**
224    * Concrete default builder leaf for the non-subclassed {@code create()} path (CRTP terminal).
225    */
226   public static final class DefaultBuilder extends Builder<DefaultBuilder> {
227
228      DefaultBuilder(Parser ctx) {
229         super(ctx);
230      }
231   }
232
233   /**
234    * Creates a new builder for this object.
235    *
236    * @param ctx The context creating this session.
237    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
238    * @return A new builder.
239    */
240   @SuppressWarnings({
241      "java:S1452" // Builder<?> wildcard return intentional; callers use it to construct session instances polymorphically
242   })
243   public static Builder<?> create(Parser ctx) {
244      return new DefaultBuilder(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
245   }
246
247   /**
248    * Convenience method for calling the {@link NameProperty @NameProperty} method on the specified object if it exists.
249    *
250    * @param cm The class type of the object.
251    * @param o The object.
252    * @param name The name to set.
253    * @throws ExecutableException Exception occurred on invoked constructor/method/field.
254    */
255   protected static final void setName(ClassMeta<?> cm, Object o, Object name) throws ExecutableException {
256      if (nn(cm)) {
257         Property m = cm.getNameProperty();
258         if (nn(m) && m.canWrite())
259            m.set(o, name);
260      }
261   }
262
263   /**
264    * Convenience method for calling the {@link ParentProperty @ParentProperty} method on the specified object if it
265    * exists.
266    *
267    * @param cm The class type of the object.
268    * @param o The object.
269    * @param parent The parent to set.
270    * @throws ExecutableException Exception occurred on invoked constructor/method/field.
271    */
272   protected static final void setParent(ClassMeta<?> cm, Object o, Object parent) throws ExecutableException {
273      if (cm == null)
274         return;
275      Property m = cm.getParentProperty();
276      if (nn(m) && m.canWrite())
277         m.set(o, parent);
278   }
279
280   private final HttpPartSchema schema;
281   private final Method javaMethod;
282   private final Object outer;
283   private final boolean trimStrings;
284   private final Nulls nulls;
285   private final Parser ctx;
286   private final ParserListener listener;
287   private final Deque<StringBuilder> sbStack;
288   private BeanPropertyMeta currentProperty;
289   private ClassMeta<?> currentClass;
290   private Position mark = new Position(-1);
291   private ParserPipe pipe;
292
293   /**
294    * Constructor.
295    *
296    * @param builder The builder for this object.
297    */
298   protected ParserSession(Builder<?> builder) {
299      super(builder);
300      ctx = builder.ctx;
301      javaMethod = builder.javaMethod;
302      outer = builder.outer;
303      schema = builder.schema;
304      trimStrings = builder.trimStrings;
305      nulls = builder.nulls == null ? Nulls.NOT_SET : builder.nulls;
306      listener = BeanInstantiator.createOrNull(ctx.getListener());
307      sbStack = new ArrayDeque<>();
308   }
309
310   /**
311    * Returns the input as a string.
312    *
313    * <p>
314    * This always returns a value for input of type {@link CharSequence}.
315    * <br>For other input types, use {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.Context.Builder#debug()} setting to enable caching to a string
316    * before parsing so that this method returns the input.
317    *
318    * @return The input as a string, or <jk>null</jk> if no pipe has been created or we're reading from an uncached reader or input stream source.
319    */
320   public String getInputAsString() { return pipe == null ? null : pipe.getInputAsString(); }
321
322   /**
323    * Returns information used to determine at what location in the parse a failure occurred.
324    *
325    * @return A map, typically containing something like <c>{line:123,column:456,currentProperty:"foobar"}</c>
326    */
327   public final JsonMap getLastLocation() {
328      var m = new JsonMap();
329      if (nn(currentClass))
330         m.put("currentClass", currentClass.toString(true));
331      if (nn(currentProperty))
332         m.put("currentProperty", currentProperty);
333      return m;
334   }
335
336   /**
337    * Returns the listener associated with this session.
338    *
339    * @return The listener associated with this session, or <jk>null</jk> if there is no listener.
340    */
341   public ParserListener getListener() { return listener; }
342
343   /**
344    * Returns the listener associated with this session.
345    *
346    * @param <T> The listener type.
347    * @param c The listener class to cast to.
348    * @return The listener associated with this session, or <jk>null</jk> if there is no listener.
349    */
350   public <T extends ParserListener> T getListener(Class<T> c) {
351      return (T)listener;
352   }
353
354   /**
355    * Returns the current position into the reader or input stream.
356    *
357    * @return
358    *    The current position into the reader or input stream.
359    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
360    */
361   public Position getPosition() {
362      if (mark.line != -1 || mark.column != -1 || mark.position != -1)
363         return mark;
364      if (pipe == null)
365         return Position.UNKNOWN;
366      return pipe.getPosition();
367   }
368
369   /**
370    * HTTP part schema of object being parsed.
371    *
372    * @return HTTP part schema of object being parsed, or <jk>null</jk> if not specified.
373    */
374   public final HttpPartSchema getSchema() { return schema; }
375
376   /**
377    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this parser subclasses from {@link ReaderParser}.
378    *
379    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this parser subclasses from {@link ReaderParser}.
380    */
381   public boolean isReaderParser() { return false; }
382
383   /**
384    * Same as {@link #parse(Object, Type, Type...)} except optimized for a non-parameterized class.
385    *
386    * <p>
387    * This is the preferred parse method for simple types since you don't need to cast the results.
388    *
389    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
390    * <p class='bjava'>
391    *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>;
392    *
393    *    <jc>// Parse into a string.</jc>
394    *    String <jv>string</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
395    *
396    *    <jc>// Parse into a bean.</jc>
397    *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
398    *
399    *    <jc>// Parse into a bean array.</jc>
400    *    MyBean[] <jv>beanArray</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, MyBean[].<jk>class</jk>);
401    *
402    *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of objects.</jc>
403    *    List <jv>list</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>);
404    *
405    *    <jc>// Parse into a map of object keys/values.</jc>
406    *    Map <jv>map</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, TreeMap.<jk>class</jk>);
407    * </p>
408    *
409    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
410    * @param input
411    *    The input.
412    *    See {@link #parse(Object, Type, Type...)} for details.
413    * @param type The object type to create.
414    * @return The parsed object.
415    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
416    * @throws IOException Thrown by the underlying stream.
417    */
418   public final <T> T parse(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
419      try (var p = createPipe(input)) {
420         return parseInner(p, getClassMeta(type));
421      }
422   }
423
424   /**
425    * Same as {@link #parse(Object, Type, Type...)} except the type has already been converted into a {@link ClassMeta}
426    * object.
427    *
428    * <p>
429    * This is mostly an internal method used by the framework.
430    *
431    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
432    * @param input
433    *    The input.
434    *    See {@link #parse(Object, Type, Type...)} for details.
435    * @param type The object type to create.
436    * @return The parsed object.
437    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
438    * @throws IOException Thrown by the underlying stream.
439    */
440   public final <T> T parse(Object input, ClassMeta<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
441      try (var p = createPipe(input)) {
442         return parseInner(p, type);
443      }
444   }
445
446   /**
447    * Parses input into the specified object type.
448    *
449    * <p>
450    * The type can be a simple type (e.g. beans, strings, numbers) or parameterized type (collections/maps).
451    *
452    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
453    * <p class='bjava'>
454    *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>;
455    *
456    *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of strings.</jc>
457    *    List <jv>list1</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
458    *
459    *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of beans.</jc>
460    *    List <jv>list2</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
461    *
462    *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of linked-lists of strings.</jc>
463    *    List <jv>list3</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
464    *
465    *    <jc>// Parse into a map of string keys/values.</jc>
466    *    Map <jv>map1</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, TreeMap.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
467    *
468    *    <jc>// Parse into a map containing string keys and values of lists containing beans.</jc>
469    *    Map <jv>map2</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, TreeMap.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, List.<jk>class</jk>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
470    * </p>
471    *
472    * <p>
473    * <c>Collection</c> classes are assumed to be followed by zero or one objects indicating the element type.
474    *
475    * <p>
476    * <c>Map</c> classes are assumed to be followed by zero or two meta objects indicating the key and value types.
477    *
478    * <p>
479    * The array can be arbitrarily long to indicate arbitrarily complex data structures.
480    *
481    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
482    *    <li class='note'>
483    *       Use the {@link #parse(Object, Class)} method instead if you don't need a parameterized map/collection.
484    * </ul>
485    *
486    * @param <T> The class type of the object to create.
487    * @param input
488    *    The input.
489    *    <br>Character-based parsers can handle the following input class types:
490    *    <ul>
491    *       <li><jk>null</jk>
492    *       <li>{@link Reader}
493    *       <li>{@link CharSequence}
494    *       <li>{@link InputStream} containing UTF-8 encoded text (or charset defined by
495    *          {@link ReaderParser.Builder#streamCharset(Charset)} property value).
496    *       <li><code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code> containing UTF-8 encoded text (or charset defined by
497    *          {@link ReaderParser.Builder#streamCharset(Charset)} property value).
498    *       <li>{@link File} containing system encoded text (or charset defined by
499    *          {@link ReaderParser.Builder#fileCharset(Charset)} property value).
500    *    </ul>
501    *    <br>Stream-based parsers can handle the following input class types:
502    *    <ul>
503    *       <li><jk>null</jk>
504    *       <li>{@link InputStream}
505    *       <li><code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>
506    *       <li>{@link File}
507    *    </ul>
508    * @param type
509    *    The object type to create.
510    *    <br>Can be any of the following: {@link ClassMeta}, {@link Class}, {@link ParameterizedType}, {@link GenericArrayType}
511    * @param args
512    *    The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
513    *    <br>Can be any of the following: {@link ClassMeta}, {@link Class}, {@link ParameterizedType}, {@link GenericArrayType}
514    *    <br>Ignored if the main type is not a map or collection.
515    * @return The parsed object.
516    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
517    * @see MarshallingSession#getClassMeta(Type,Type...) for argument syntax for maps and collections.
518    * @throws IOException Thrown by the underlying stream.
519    */
520   public final <T> T parse(Object input, Type type, Type...args) throws ParseException, IOException {
521      try (var p = createPipe(input)) {
522         return (T)parseInner(p, getClassMeta(type, args));
523      }
524   }
525
526   /**
527    * Same as {@link #parse(Object, Class)} but parses from a string and doesn't throw an {@link IOException}.
528    *
529    * <p>
530    * This is the preferred parse method for simple types since you don't need to cast the results.
531    *
532    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
533    * <p class='bjava'>
534    *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>;
535    *
536    *    <jc>// Parse into a string.</jc>
537    *    String <jv>string</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
538    *
539    *    <jc>// Parse into a bean.</jc>
540    *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
541    *
542    *    <jc>// Parse into a bean array.</jc>
543    *    MyBean[] <jv>beanArray</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, MyBean[].<jk>class</jk>);
544    *
545    *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of objects.</jc>
546    *    List <jv>list</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>);
547    *
548    *    <jc>// Parse into a map of object keys/values.</jc>
549    *    Map <jv>map</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, TreeMap.<jk>class</jk>);
550    * </p>
551    *
552    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
553    * @param input
554    *    The input.
555    *    See {@link #parse(Object, Type, Type...)} for details.
556    * @param type The object type to create.
557    * @return The parsed object.
558    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
559    */
560   public final <T> T parse(String input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException {
561      try (var p = createPipe(input)) {
562         return parseInner(p, getClassMeta(type));
563      } catch (IOException e) {
564         throw new ParseException(e); // Shouldn't happen.
565      }
566   }
567
568   /**
569    * Same as {@link #parse(Object, ClassMeta)} except parses from a string and doesn't throw an {@link IOException}.
570    *
571    * <p>
572    * This is mostly an internal method used by the framework.
573    *
574    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
575    * @param input
576    *    The input.
577    *    See {@link #parse(Object, Type, Type...)} for details.
578    * @param type The object type to create.
579    * @return The parsed object.
580    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
581    */
582   public final <T> T parse(String input, ClassMeta<T> type) throws ParseException {
583      try (var p = createPipe(input)) {
584         return parseInner(p, type);
585      } catch (IOException e) {
586         throw new ParseException(e); // Shouldn't happen.
587      }
588   }
589
590   /**
591    * Same as {@link #parse(Object,Type,Type...)} but parses from a string and doesn't throw an {@link IOException}.
592    *
593    * @param <T> The class type of the object to create.
594    * @param input
595    *    The input.
596    *    <br>Character-based parsers can handle the following input class types:
597    *    <ul>
598    *       <li><jk>null</jk>
599    *       <li>{@link Reader}
600    *       <li>{@link CharSequence}
601    *       <li>{@link InputStream} containing UTF-8 encoded text (or charset defined by
602    *          {@link ReaderParser.Builder#streamCharset(Charset)} property value).
603    *       <li><code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code> containing UTF-8 encoded text (or charset defined by
604    *          {@link ReaderParser.Builder#streamCharset(Charset)} property value).
605    *       <li>{@link File} containing system encoded text (or charset defined by
606    *          {@link ReaderParser.Builder#fileCharset(Charset)} property value).
607    *    </ul>
608    *    <br>Stream-based parsers can handle the following input class types:
609    *    <ul>
610    *       <li><jk>null</jk>
611    *       <li>{@link InputStream}
612    *       <li><code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>
613    *       <li>{@link File}
614    *    </ul>
615    * @param type
616    *    The object type to create.
617    *    <br>Can be any of the following: {@link ClassMeta}, {@link Class}, {@link ParameterizedType}, {@link GenericArrayType}
618    * @param args
619    *    The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
620    *    <br>Can be any of the following: {@link ClassMeta}, {@link Class}, {@link ParameterizedType}, {@link GenericArrayType}
621    *    <br>Ignored if the main type is not a map or collection.
622    * @return The parsed object.
623    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
624    * @see MarshallingSession#getClassMeta(Type,Type...) for argument syntax for maps and collections.
625    */
626   public final <T> T parse(String input, Type type, Type...args) throws ParseException {
627      try (var p = createPipe(input)) {
628         return (T)parseInner(p, getClassMeta(type, args));
629      } catch (IOException e) {
630         throw new ParseException(e); // Shouldn't happen.
631      }
632   }
633
634   /**
635    * Parses the specified array input with each entry in the object defined by the {@code argTypes}
636    * argument.
637    *
638    * <p>
639    * Used for converting arrays (e.g. <js>"[arg1,arg2,...]"</js>) into an {@code Object[]} that can be passed
640    * to the {@code Method.invoke(target, args)} method.
641    *
642    * <p>
643    * Used in the following locations:
644    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
645    *    <li>
646    *       Used to parse argument strings in the {@link ObjectIntrospector#invokeMethod(Method, Reader)} method.
647    * </ul>
648    *
649    * @param input The input.  Subclasses can support different input types.
650    * @param argTypes Specifies the type of objects to create for each entry in the array.
651    * @return An array of parsed objects.
652    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
653    */
654   public final Object[] parseArgs(Object input, Type[] argTypes) throws ParseException {
655      try (var p = createPipe(input)) {
656         return doParse(p, getArgsClassMeta(argTypes));
657      } catch (ParseException e) {
658         throw e;
659      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") StackOverflowError e) {
660         throw new ParseException(this, "Depth too deep.  Stack overflow occurred.");
661      } catch (IOException e) {
662         throw new ParseException(this, e, "I/O exception occurred.  exception={0}, message={1}.", cns(e), localizedMessage(e));
663      } catch (Exception e) {
664         throw new ParseException(this, e, "Exception occurred.  exception={0}, message={1}.", cns(e), localizedMessage(e));
665      } finally {
666         checkForWarnings();
667      }
668   }
669
670   /**
671    * Parses the contents of the specified reader and loads the results into the specified collection.
672    *
673    * <p>
674    * Used in the following locations:
675    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
676    *    <li>
677    *       The various character-based constructors in {@link JsonList} (e.g.
678    *       {@link JsonList#JsonList(CharSequence,Parser)}.
679    * </ul>
680    *
681    * @param <E> The element class type.
682    * @param input The input.  See {@link #parse(Object, ClassMeta)} for supported input types.
683    * @param c The collection being loaded.
684    * @param elementType The class type of the elements, or <jk>null</jk> to default to whatever is being parsed.
685    * @return The same collection that was passed in to allow this method to be chained.
686    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
687    * @throws UnsupportedOperationException If not implemented.
688    */
689   public final <E> Collection<E> parseIntoCollection(Object input, Collection<E> c, Type elementType) throws ParseException {
690      try (var p = createPipe(input)) {
691         return doParseIntoCollection(p, c, elementType);
692      } catch (ParseException e) {
693         throw e;
694      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") StackOverflowError e) {
695         throw new ParseException(this, "Depth too deep.  Stack overflow occurred.");
696      } catch (IOException e) {
697         throw new ParseException(this, e, "I/O exception occurred.  exception={0}, message={1}.", cns(e), localizedMessage(e));
698      } catch (Exception e) {
699         throw new ParseException(this, e, "Exception occurred.  exception={0}, message={1}.", cns(e), localizedMessage(e));
700      } finally {
701         checkForWarnings();
702      }
703   }
704
705   /**
706    * Parses the contents of the specified reader and loads the results into the specified map.
707    *
708    * <p>
709    * Reader must contain something that serializes to a map (such as text containing a JSON object).
710    *
711    * <p>
712    * Used in the following locations:
713    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
714    *    <li>
715    *       The various character-based constructors in {@link JsonMap} (e.g.
716    *       {@link JsonMap#JsonMap(CharSequence,Parser)}).
717    * </ul>
718    *
719    * @param <K> The key class type.
720    * @param <V> The value class type.
721    * @param input The input.  See {@link #parse(Object, ClassMeta)} for supported input types.
722    * @param m The map being loaded.
723    * @param keyType The class type of the keys, or <jk>null</jk> to default to <code>String.<jk>class</jk></code>.
724    * @param valueType The class type of the values, or <jk>null</jk> to default to whatever is being parsed.
725    * @return The same map that was passed in to allow this method to be chained.
726    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
727    * @throws UnsupportedOperationException If not implemented.
728    */
729   public final <K,V> Map<K,V> parseIntoMap(Object input, Map<K,V> m, Type keyType, Type valueType) throws ParseException {
730      try (var p = createPipe(input)) {
731         return doParseIntoMap(p, m, keyType, valueType);
732      } catch (ParseException e) {
733         throw e;
734      } catch (Exception e) {
735         throw new ParseException(this, e);
736      } finally {
737         checkForWarnings();
738      }
739   }
740
741   /**
742    * Entry point for all parsing calls.
743    *
744    * <p>
745    * Calls the {@link #doParse(ParserPipe, ClassMeta)} implementation class and catches/re-wraps any exceptions
746    * thrown.
747    *
748    * @param pipe The parser input.
749    * @param type The class type of the object to create.
750    * @param <T> The class type of the object to create.
751    * @return The parsed object.
752    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
753    * @throws IOException Thrown by the underlying stream.
754    */
755   private <T> T parseInner(ParserPipe pipe, ClassMeta<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
756      if (type.isVoid())
757         return null;
758      if (BeanSupplier.class.isAssignableFrom(type.inner()) && !BeanChannel.class.isAssignableFrom(type.inner()))
759         throw new ParseException(this, "BeanSupplier cannot be used as a parser target. Use BeanConsumer or BeanChannel for round-trip support.");
760      try {
761         var r = doParse(pipe, type);
762         // Primitive optionals (OptionalInt/Long/Double) are parsed as first-class optionals — the format
763         // parsers wrap the element in a generic Optional, so coerce the top-level result to the matching
764         // primitive-optional type (mirrors the Optional<T> contract; absent/null already resolved to empty).
765         if (type.isPrimitiveOptional() && ! type.inner().isInstance(r))
766            return convertToType(r, type);
767         return r;
768      } catch (ParseException | IOException e) {
769         throw e;
770      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") StackOverflowError e) {
771         throw new ParseException(this, "Depth too deep.  Stack overflow occurred.");
772      } catch (Exception e) {
773         throw new ParseException(this, e, "Exception occurred.  exception={0}, message={1}.", cns(e), localizedMessage(e));
774      } finally {
775         checkForWarnings();
776      }
777   }
778
779   /**
780    * Parses the input and streams each parsed element into the given {@link BeanConsumer}.
781    *
782    * <p>
783    * This is the primary API for large-dataset parsing where parsed beans should be consumed lazily
784    * (e.g. inserted directly into a database) rather than collected into an in-memory collection.
785    *
786    * <p>
787    * The parser drives the full consumer lifecycle:
788    * <ol>
789    *    <li>Calls {@link BeanConsumer#begin()} before parsing starts
790    *    <li>Calls {@link BeanConsumer#acceptThrows(Object)} for each parsed element
791    *    <li>If {@link BeanConsumer#acceptThrows(Object)} throws, calls {@link BeanConsumer#onError(Exception)};
792    *        if {@code onError()} absorbs the exception, parsing continues to the next element
793    *    <li>Always calls {@link BeanConsumer#complete()} at the end (like {@code finally})
794    * </ol>
795    *
796    * <p>
797    * The default implementation parses the input into a {@link List} first and then feeds each element
798    * to the consumer. Format-specific subclasses may override {@link #doParseToBeanConsumer} for true
799    * streaming behavior without loading all elements into memory.
800    *
801    * @param <T> The element type.
802    * @param input The input to parse.
803    * @param consumer The consumer to receive parsed elements.
804    * @param elementType The type of each element.
805    * @throws ParseException If a parse error occurs that causes {@code onError()} to rethrow.
806    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs reading the input.
807    */
808   public final <T> void parseToBeanConsumer(Object input, BeanConsumer<T> consumer, Class<T> elementType) throws ParseException, IOException {
809      try (var p = createPipe(input)) {
810         doParseToBeanConsumer(p, consumer, elementType);
811      }
812   }
813
814   /**
815    * Format-specific implementation for {@link #parseToBeanConsumer}.
816    *
817    * <p>
818    * The default implementation parses the input as a {@link List} and feeds each element to the consumer.
819    * Override this method in format-specific parsers to support true streaming without collecting all
820    * elements into memory.
821    *
822    * @param <T> The element type.
823    * @param pipe The parser input pipe.
824    * @param consumer The consumer to receive parsed elements.
825    * @param elementType The type of each element.
826    * @throws ParseException If a parse error occurs.
827    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs.
828    */
829   protected <T> void doParseToBeanConsumer(ParserPipe pipe, BeanConsumer<T> consumer, Class<T> elementType) throws ParseException, IOException {
830      List<T> list = (List<T>) doParse(pipe, getClassMeta(List.class, elementType));
831      if (list == null)
832         return;
833      var bodyThrown = drainToConsumer(consumer, list);
834      var completeThrown = completeQuietly(consumer);
835      if (bodyThrown != null) {
836         if (completeThrown != null)
837            bodyThrown.addSuppressed(completeThrown);
838         if (bodyThrown instanceof ParseException e)
839            throw e;
840         if (bodyThrown instanceof IOException e)
841            throw e;
842         throw new ParseException(this, bodyThrown, "Exception occurred.  exception={0}, message={1}.", cns(bodyThrown), localizedMessage(bodyThrown));
843      }
844      if (completeThrown != null)
845         throw new ParseException(this, completeThrown, "Exception occurred in BeanConsumer.complete().  exception={0}, message={1}.", cns(completeThrown), localizedMessage(completeThrown));
846   }
847
848   private static <T> Exception drainToConsumer(BeanConsumer<T> consumer, List<T> list) {
849      try {
850         consumer.begin();
851         for (var element : list)
852            acceptOrError(consumer, element);
853      } catch (Exception e) {
854         return e;
855      }
856      return null;
857   }
858
859   private static <T> void acceptOrError(BeanConsumer<T> consumer, T element) throws Exception {
860      try {
861         consumer.acceptThrows(element);
862      } catch (Exception e) {
863         consumer.onError(e);
864      }
865   }
866
867   private static Exception completeQuietly(BeanConsumer<?> consumer) {
868      try {
869         consumer.complete();
870         return null;
871      } catch (Exception e) {
872         return e;
873      }
874   }
875
876   /**
877    * Converts the specified {@link MarshalledMap} into a bean identified by the <js>"_type"</js> property in the map.
878    *
879    * @param m The map to convert to a bean.
880    * @param pMeta The current bean property being parsed.
881    * @param eType The current expected type being parsed.
882    * @return
883    *    The converted bean, or the same map if the <js>"_type"</js> entry wasn't found or didn't resolve to a bean.
884    */
885   protected final Object cast(MarshalledMap m, BeanPropertyMeta pMeta, ClassMeta<?> eType) {
886
887      var btpn = getBeanTypePropertyName(eType);
888
889      var o = m.get(btpn);
890      if (o == null)
891         return m;
892      var typeName = o.toString();
893
894      var cm = getClassMeta(typeName, pMeta, eType);
895
896      if (nn(cm)) {
897         var bm = m.getMarshallingSession().newBeanMap(cm.inner());
898
899         // Iterate through all the entries in the map and set the individual field values.
900         m.forEach((k, v) -> {
901            if (! k.equals(btpn)) {
902               // Attempt to recursively cast child maps.
903               if (v instanceof MarshalledMap v2)
904                  v = cast(v2, pMeta, eType);
905               bm.put(k, v);
906            }
907         });
908         return bm.getBean();
909      }
910
911      return m;
912   }
913
914   /**
915    * Converts the specified string to the specified type.
916    *
917    * @param outer
918    *    The outer object if we're converting to an inner object that needs to be created within the context
919    *    of an outer object.
920    * @param s The string to convert.
921    * @param type The class type to convert the string to.
922    * @return The string converted as an object of the specified type.
923    * @param <T> The class type to convert the string to.
924    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
925    * @throws ExecutableException Exception occurred on invoked constructor/method/field.
926    */
927   @SuppressWarnings({
928      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for type-conversion dispatch across all supported target types
929   })
930   protected final <T> T convertAttrToType(Object outer, String s, ClassMeta<T> type) throws ParseException {
931      if (s == null)
932         return null;
933
934      if (type == null)
935         type = (ClassMeta<T>)object();
936      var swap = type.getSwap(this);
937      var sType = swap == null ? type : swap.getSwapClassMeta(this);
938
939      var o = (Object)s;
940      if (sType.isChar())
941         o = parseCharacter(s);
942      else if (sType.isNumber())
943         o = parseNumber(s, (Class<? extends Number>)sType.inner());
944      else if (sType.isBoolean())
945         o = Boolean.parseBoolean(s);
946      else if (sType.isDate())
947         o = parseDate(s, sType);
948      else if (sType.isCalendar())
949         o = parseCalendar(s, sType);
950      else if (sType.isTemporal())
951         o = parseTemporal(s, sType);
952      else if (sType.isDuration())
953         o = parseDuration(s);
954      else if (sType.isPeriod())
955         o = parsePeriod(s);
956      else if (! (sType.isCharSequence() || sType.isObject())) {
957         if (sType.canCreateNewInstanceFromString(outer))
958            o = sType.newInstanceFromString(outer, s);
959         else
960            throw new ParseException(this, "Invalid conversion from string to class ''{0}''", type);
961      }
962
963      if (nn(swap))
964         o = unswap(swap, o, type);
965
966      return (T)o;
967   }
968
969   /**
970    * Wraps the specified input object into a {@link ParserPipe} object so that it can be easily converted into
971    * a stream or reader.
972    *
973    * @param input
974    *    The input.
975    *    <br>For character-based parsers, this can be any of the following types:
976    *    <ul>
977    *       <li><jk>null</jk>
978    *       <li>{@link Reader}
979    *       <li>{@link CharSequence}
980    *       <li>{@link InputStream} containing UTF-8 encoded text (or whatever the encoding specified by
981    *          {@link ReaderParser.Builder#streamCharset(Charset)}).
982    *       <li><code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code> containing UTF-8 encoded text (or whatever the encoding specified by
983    *          {@link ReaderParser.Builder#streamCharset(Charset)}).
984    *       <li>{@link File} containing system encoded text (or whatever the encoding specified by
985    *          {@link ReaderParser.Builder#fileCharset(Charset)}).
986    *    </ul>
987    *    <br>For byte-based parsers, this can be any of the following types:
988    *    <ul>
989    *       <li><jk>null</jk>
990    *       <li>{@link InputStream}
991    *       <li><code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>
992    *       <li>{@link File}
993    *       <li>{@link CharSequence} containing encoded bytes according to the {@link InputStreamParser.Builder#binaryFormat(BinaryFormat)} setting.
994    *    </ul>
995    * @return
996    *    A new {@link ParserPipe} wrapper around the specified input object.
997    */
998   protected ParserPipe createPipe(Object input) {
999      return null;
1000   }
1001
1002   /**
1003    * Workhorse method.
1004    *
1005    * <p>
1006    * Subclasses are expected to implement this method or {@link Parser#doParse(ParserSession,ParserPipe,ClassMeta)}.
1007    *
1008    * <p>
1009    * The default implementation of this method simply calls {@link Parser#doParse(ParserSession,ParserPipe,ClassMeta)}.
1010    *
1011    * @param pipe Where to get the input from.
1012    * @param type
1013    *    The class type of the object to create.
1014    *    If <jk>null</jk> or <code>Object.<jk>class</jk></code>, object type is based on what's being parsed.
1015    *    For example, when parsing JSON text, it may return a <c>String</c>, <c>Number</c>,
1016    *    <c>JsonMap</c>, etc...
1017    * @param <T> The class type of the object to create.
1018    * @return The parsed object.
1019    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
1020    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
1021    * @throws ExecutableException Exception occurred on invoked constructor/method/field.
1022    */
1023   protected <T> T doParse(ParserPipe pipe, ClassMeta<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException, ExecutableException {
1024      return ctx.doParse(this, pipe, type);
1025   }
1026
1027   /**
1028    * Implementation method.
1029    *
1030    * <p>
1031    * Default implementation throws an {@link UnsupportedOperationException}.
1032    *
1033    * @param <E> The element type.
1034    * @param pipe The parser input.
1035    * @param c The collection being loaded.
1036    * @param elementType The class type of the elements, or <jk>null</jk> to default to whatever is being parsed.
1037    * @return The same collection that was passed in to allow this method to be chained.
1038    * @throws Exception If thrown from underlying stream, or if the input contains a syntax error or is malformed.
1039    */
1040   @SuppressWarnings({
1041      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
1042   })
1043   protected <E> Collection<E> doParseIntoCollection(ParserPipe pipe, Collection<E> c, Type elementType) throws Exception {
1044      throw uoex("Parser ''{0}'' does not support this method.", cn(getClass()));
1045   }
1046
1047   /**
1048    * Implementation method.
1049    *
1050    * <p>
1051    * Default implementation throws an {@link UnsupportedOperationException}.
1052    *
1053    * @param <K> The key type.
1054    * @param <V> The value type.
1055    * @param pipe The parser input.
1056    * @param m The map being loaded.
1057    * @param keyType The class type of the keys, or <jk>null</jk> to default to <code>String.<jk>class</jk></code>.
1058    * @param valueType The class type of the values, or <jk>null</jk> to default to whatever is being parsed.
1059    * @return The same map that was passed in to allow this method to be chained.
1060    * @throws Exception If thrown from underlying stream, or if the input contains a syntax error or is malformed.
1061    */
1062   @SuppressWarnings({
1063      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
1064   })
1065   protected <K,V> Map<K,V> doParseIntoMap(ParserPipe pipe, Map<K,V> m, Type keyType, Type valueType) throws Exception {
1066      throw uoex("Parser ''{0}'' does not support this method.", cn(getClass()));
1067   }
1068
1069   /**
1070    * Give the specified dictionary name, resolve it to a class.
1071    *
1072    * @param typeName The dictionary name to resolve.
1073    * @param pMeta The bean property we're currently parsing.
1074    * @param eType The expected type we're currently parsing.
1075    * @return The resolved class, or <jk>null</jk> if the type name could not be resolved.
1076    */
1077   @SuppressWarnings({
1078      "java:S1452"  // Wildcard required - ClassMeta<?> for type resolution
1079   })
1080   protected final ClassMeta<?> getClassMeta(String typeName, BeanPropertyMeta pMeta, ClassMeta<?> eType) {
1081      BeanRegistry br = null;
1082
1083      // Resolve via @MarshalledProp(dictionary={})
1084      if (nn(pMeta)) {
1085         br = (BeanRegistry) pMeta.getBeanRegistry();
1086         if (nn(br) && br.hasName(typeName))
1087            return br.getClassMeta(typeName);
1088      }
1089
1090      // Resolve via @Marshalled(dictionary={}) on the expected type where the
1091      // expected type is an interface with subclasses.
1092      if (nn(eType)) {
1093         br = eType.getBeanRegistry();
1094         if (nn(br) && br.hasName(typeName))
1095            return br.getClassMeta(typeName);
1096      }
1097
1098      // Last resort, resolve using the session registry.
1099      return getBeanRegistry().getClassMeta(typeName);
1100   }
1101
1102   /**
1103    * Debug output lines.
1104    *
1105    * @see Parser.Builder#debugOutputLines(int)
1106    * @return
1107    *    The number of lines of input before and after the error location to be printed as part of the exception message.
1108    */
1109   protected final int getDebugOutputLines() { return ctx.getDebugOutputLines(); }
1110
1111   /**
1112    * Returns the Java method that invoked this parser.
1113    *
1114    * <p>
1115    * When using the REST API, this is the Java method invoked by the REST call.
1116    * Can be used to access annotations defined on the method or class.
1117    *
1118    * @return The Java method that invoked this parser.
1119   */
1120   protected final Method getJavaMethod() { return javaMethod; }
1121
1122   /**
1123    * Parser listener.
1124    *
1125    * @see Parser.Builder#listener(Class)
1126    * @return
1127    *    Class used to listen for errors and warnings that occur during parsing.
1128    */
1129   protected final Class<? extends ParserListener> getListenerClass() { return ctx.getListener(); }
1130
1131   /**
1132    * Returns the outer object used for instantiating top-level non-static member classes.
1133    *
1134    * <p>
1135    * When using the REST API, this is the servlet object.
1136    *
1137    * @return The outer object.
1138   */
1139   protected final Object getOuter() { return outer; }
1140
1141   /**
1142    * Creates a reusable {@link StringBuilder} object from an internal pool.
1143    *
1144    * <p>
1145    * String builders are returned to the pool by calling {@link #returnStringBuilder(StringBuilder)}.
1146    *
1147    * @return A new or previously returned string builder.
1148    */
1149   protected final StringBuilder getStringBuilder() {
1150      if (sbStack.isEmpty())
1151         return new StringBuilder();
1152      return sbStack.pop();
1153   }
1154
1155   /**
1156    * Auto-close streams.
1157    *
1158    * @see Parser.Builder#autoCloseStreams()
1159    * @return
1160    *    <jk>true</jk> if <l>InputStreams</l> and <l>Readers</l> passed into parsers will be closed
1161    *    after parsing is complete.
1162    */
1163   protected final boolean isAutoCloseStreams() { return ctx.isAutoCloseStreams(); }
1164
1165   /**
1166    * Trim parsed strings.
1167    *
1168    * @see Parser.Builder#trimStrings()
1169    * @return
1170    *    <jk>true</jk> if string values will be trimmed of whitespace using {@link String#trim()} before being added to
1171    *    the POJO.
1172    */
1173   protected final boolean isTrimStrings() { return trimStrings; }
1174
1175   /**
1176    * Returns the effective {@link Nulls} default for this session — the per-session override (set via
1177    * {@link ParserSession.Builder#nulls(Nulls)}) when present, otherwise the parser context's
1178    * {@link Parser#getNulls()} value.
1179    *
1180    * @return The current session-level value (never <jk>null</jk>; {@link Nulls#NOT_SET} when unconfigured).
1181    * @since 10.0.0
1182    */
1183   public final Nulls getNulls() { return nulls; }
1184
1185   /**
1186    * Unbuffered.
1187    *
1188    * @see Parser.Builder#unbuffered()
1189    * @return
1190    *    <jk>true</jk> if parsers don't use internal buffering during parsing.
1191    */
1192   protected final boolean isUnbuffered() { return ctx.isUnbuffered(); }
1193
1194   /**
1195    * Duration parse hint format.
1196    *
1197    * @return The configured duration parse hint format.
1198    */
1199   protected final DurationFormat getDurationFormat() { return ctx.getMarshallingContext().getDurationFormat(); }
1200
1201   /**
1202    * Period parse hint format.
1203    *
1204    * @return The configured period parse hint format.
1205    */
1206   protected final PeriodFormat getPeriodFormat() { return ctx.getMarshallingContext().getPeriodFormat(); }
1207
1208   /**
1209    * Calendar parse hint format.
1210    *
1211    * @return The configured calendar parse hint format.
1212    */
1213   protected final CalendarFormat getCalendarFormat() { return ctx.getMarshallingContext().getCalendarFormat(); }
1214
1215   /**
1216    * Date parse hint format.
1217    *
1218    * @return The configured date parse hint format.
1219    */
1220   protected final DateFormat getDateFormat() { return ctx.getMarshallingContext().getDateFormat(); }
1221
1222   /**
1223    * Temporal parse hint format.
1224    *
1225    * @return The configured temporal parse hint format.
1226    */
1227   protected final TemporalFormat getTemporalFormat() { return ctx.getMarshallingContext().getTemporalFormat(); }
1228
1229   /**
1230    * Time-zone parse hint format.
1231    *
1232    * @return The configured time-zone parse hint format.
1233    */
1234   protected final TimeZoneFormat getTimeZoneFormat() { return ctx.getMarshallingContext().getTimeZoneFormat(); }
1235
1236   /**
1237    * Locale parse hint format.
1238    *
1239    * @return The configured locale parse hint format.
1240    */
1241   protected final LocaleFormat getLocaleFormat() { return ctx.getMarshallingContext().getLocaleFormat(); }
1242
1243   /**
1244    * Parses an ISO 8601 / numeric wire value into a {@link Duration} using this session's configured
1245    * {@linkplain #getDurationFormat() duration format} as the disambiguation hint.
1246    *
1247    * @param s The wire value to parse. <jk>null</jk> or empty returns <jk>null</jk>.
1248    * @return The parsed {@link Duration}, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code s} is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
1249    */
1250   protected final Duration parseDuration(String s) {
1251      return Iso8601Utils.parseDuration(s, getDurationFormat());
1252   }
1253
1254   /**
1255    * Parses an ISO 8601 / numeric wire value into a {@link Period} using this session's configured
1256    * {@linkplain #getPeriodFormat() period format} as the disambiguation hint.
1257    *
1258    * @param s The wire value to parse. <jk>null</jk> or empty returns <jk>null</jk>.
1259    * @return The parsed {@link Period}, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code s} is <jk>null</jk> or empty.
1260    */
1261   protected final Period parsePeriod(String s) {
1262      return Iso8601Utils.parsePeriod(s, getPeriodFormat());
1263   }
1264
1265   /**
1266    * Parses an ISO 8601 wire value into a {@link Date} target using this session's configured
1267    * {@linkplain #getDateFormat() date format} and {@linkplain #getTimeZone() time zone}.
1268    *
1269    * @param <T> The target type (unbounded to match {@link Iso8601Utils#parseDate}).
1270    * @param s The wire value to parse. <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
1271    * @param cm The target class metadata.
1272    * @return The parsed value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code s} is <jk>null</jk> or the target is not a
1273    *    recognized {@link Date} type.
1274    */
1275   protected final <T> T parseDate(String s, ClassMeta<T> cm) {
1276      return Iso8601Utils.parseDate(s, cm, getDateFormat(), getTimeZone());
1277   }
1278
1279   /**
1280    * Parses an ISO 8601 wire value into a {@link Calendar} or {@link XMLGregorianCalendar}
1281    * target using this session's configured {@linkplain #getCalendarFormat() calendar format} and
1282    * {@linkplain #getTimeZone() time zone}.
1283    *
1284    * @param <T> The target type (unbounded because {@link XMLGregorianCalendar} is not a
1285    *    {@link Calendar} subclass).
1286    * @param s The wire value to parse. <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
1287    * @param cm The target class metadata.
1288    * @return The parsed value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code s} is <jk>null</jk> or the target is not a
1289    *    recognized calendar type.
1290    */
1291   protected final <T> T parseCalendar(String s, ClassMeta<T> cm) {
1292      return Iso8601Utils.parseCalendar(s, cm, getCalendarFormat(), getTimeZone());
1293   }
1294
1295   /**
1296    * Parses an ISO 8601 wire value into the specified {@link Temporal} subtype using
1297    * this session's configured {@linkplain #getTemporalFormat() temporal format} and
1298    * {@linkplain #getTimeZone() time zone}.
1299    *
1300    * @param <T> The target type (unbounded to match {@link Iso8601Utils#parseTemporal}).
1301    * @param s The wire value to parse. <jk>null</jk> returns <jk>null</jk>.
1302    * @param cm The target class metadata.
1303    * @return The parsed value, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code s} is <jk>null</jk> or the target is not a
1304    *    {@link Temporal} subtype.
1305    */
1306   protected final <T> T parseTemporal(String s, ClassMeta<T> cm) {
1307      return Iso8601Utils.parseTemporal(s, cm, getTemporalFormat(), getTimeZone());
1308   }
1309
1310   /**
1311    * Marks the current position.
1312    */
1313   protected void mark() {
1314      if (nn(pipe)) {
1315         Position p = pipe.getPosition();
1316         mark.line = p.line;
1317         mark.column = p.column;
1318         mark.position = p.position;
1319      }
1320   }
1321
1322   /**
1323    * Specialized warning when an exception is thrown while executing a bean setter.
1324    *
1325    * @param p The bean map entry representing the bean property.
1326    * @param t The throwable that the bean setter threw.
1327    */
1328   protected final void onBeanSetterException(BeanPropertyMeta p, Throwable t) {
1329      if (nn(listener))
1330         listener.onBeanSetterException(this, t, p);
1331      var prefix = "";
1332      addWarning("{0}Could not call setValue() on property ''{1}'' of class ''{2}'', exception = {3}", prefix, p.getName(), p.getBeanMeta().getBeanInfo(), localizedMessage(t));
1333   }
1334
1335   /**
1336    * Method that gets called when an unknown bean property name is encountered.
1337    *
1338    * @param propertyName The unknown bean property name.
1339    * @param beanMap The bean that doesn't have the expected property.
1340    * @param value The parsed value.
1341    * @throws ParseException
1342    *    Automatically thrown if {@link MarshallingContext.Builder#ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()} setting on this parser is
1343    *    <jk>false</jk>
1344    * @param <T> The class type of the bean map that doesn't have the expected property.
1345    */
1346   protected final <T> void onUnknownProperty(String propertyName, BeanMap<T> beanMap, Object value) throws ParseException {
1347      var bmcm = (ClassMeta<T>) beanMap.getBeanInfo();
1348      if (propertyName.equals(getBeanTypePropertyName(bmcm)))
1349         return;
1350      if (! isIgnoreUnknownBeanProperties() && (nn(value) || ! isIgnoreUnknownNullBeanProperties()))
1351         throw new ParseException(this, "Unknown property ''{0}'' encountered while trying to parse into class ''{1}''", propertyName, bmcm);
1352      if (nn(listener))
1353         listener.onUnknownBeanProperty(this, propertyName, bmcm.inner(), beanMap.getBean());
1354   }
1355
1356   @Override /* Overridden from MarshallingSession */
1357   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
1358      return super.properties()
1359         .a(PROP_javaMethod, javaMethod)
1360         .a(PROP_listener, listener)
1361         .a(PROP_outer, outer)
1362         .a(PROP_trimStrings, trimStrings)
1363         .a(PROP_nulls, nulls);
1364   }
1365
1366   /**
1367    * Returns a {@link StringBuilder} object back into the internal reuse pool.
1368    *
1369    * @param sb The string builder to return to the pool.  No-op if <jk>null</jk>.
1370    */
1371   protected final void returnStringBuilder(StringBuilder sb) {
1372      if (sb == null)
1373         return;
1374      sb.setLength(0);
1375      sbStack.push(sb);
1376   }
1377
1378   /**
1379    * Sets the current class being parsed for proper error messages.
1380    *
1381    * @param currentClass The current class being parsed.
1382    */
1383   protected final void setCurrentClass(ClassMeta<?> currentClass) { this.currentClass = currentClass; }
1384
1385   /**
1386    * Sets the current bean property being parsed for proper error messages.
1387    *
1388    * @param currentProperty The current property being parsed.
1389    */
1390   protected final void setCurrentProperty(BeanPropertyMeta currentProperty) { this.currentProperty = currentProperty; }
1391
1392   /**
1393    * The {@link #createPipe(Object)} method should call this method to set the pipe for debugging purposes.
1394    *
1395    * @param pipe The pipe created for this session.
1396    * @return The same pipe.
1397    */
1398   protected ParserPipe setPipe(ParserPipe pipe) {
1399      this.pipe = pipe;
1400      return pipe;
1401   }
1402
1403   /**
1404    * Trims the specified object if it's a <c>String</c> and {@link #isTrimStrings()} returns <jk>true</jk>.
1405    *
1406    * @param <K> The object type.
1407    * @param o The object to trim.
1408    * @return The trimmed string if it's a string.
1409    */
1410   protected final <K> K trim(K o) {
1411      if (isTrimStrings() && o instanceof String o2)
1412         return (K)o2.trim();
1413      return o;
1414
1415   }
1416
1417   /**
1418    * Trims the specified string if {@link ParserSession#isTrimStrings()} returns <jk>true</jk>.
1419    *
1420    * @param s The input string to trim.
1421    * @return The trimmed string, or <jk>null</jk> if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
1422    */
1423   protected final String trim(String s) {
1424      if (isTrimStrings() && nn(s))
1425         return s.trim();
1426      return s;
1427   }
1428
1429   /**
1430    * Unmarks the current position.
1431    */
1432   protected void unmark() {
1433      mark.line = -1;
1434      mark.column = -1;
1435      mark.position = -1;
1436   }
1437
1438   /**
1439    * Invokes the specified swap on the specified object.
1440    *
1441    * @param swap The swap to invoke.
1442    * @param o The input object.
1443    * @param eType The expected type.
1444    * @return The swapped object.
1445    * @throws ParseException If swap method threw an exception.
1446    */
1447   protected Object unswap(ObjectSwap swap, Object o, ClassMeta<?> eType) throws ParseException {
1448      try {
1449         return swap.unswap(this, o, eType);
1450      } catch (Exception e) {
1451         throw new ParseException(e);
1452      }
1453   }
1454}
